conbody.p: A virtual environment is an isolated container for a specific project. It holds its own Python version and its own set of installed packages, completely separate from everything else on your computer.

section.title: The Problem Virtual Environments Solve
section.p: Different projects often require different versions of the same package. Without isolation, installing a package for one project can break another. Virtual environments prevent this conflict by giving each project its own private space.

section.title: Analogy: A Dedicated Toolbox
section.p: Think of a virtual environment as a dedicated toolbox for a single job. The tools inside (packages) are specific to that job and do not interfere with the tools in any other toolbox.

example.title: This Course's Environment
example.p: You will create an environment called nlp-course. It will contain spaCy and all supporting packages. When you are done with the course, you can delete this environment without affecting any other software on your machine.
